source: Per-row citations below. External figures fetched 2026-06-11: Invesco QQQ annualised NAV returns as of 2026-05-31 (https://www.invesco.com/qqq-etf/en/performance.html, 'Annualized returns as of 05/31/2026' table, Invesco QQQ NAV row, read verbatim via headless browser — JS-rendered page); SPDR SPY annualised NAV returns as of 2026-05-31 (https://www.ssga.com/us/en/intermediary/etfs/spdr-sp-500-etf-trust-spy); FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI factsheet, data as at 2026-05-29 (https://research.ftserussell.com/Analytics/FactSheets/Home/DownloadSingleIssue?issueName=FBMKLCI&IsManual=false, 'Performance and Volatility - Capital Return' table); BNM USD/MYR exchange rates, 1700-session middle rates (BNM Open API, https://api.bnm.gov.my/public/exchange-rate/USD/date/{date}?session=1700&quote=rm — bnm.gov.my itself returns HTTP 403 to non-browser fetches; the Open API at api.bnm.gov.my is BNM's official machine-readable publication of the same published rates).
notes: Factsheet-published standard periods only (5y and 10y annualised, NAV-based, exactly as published; SC App.1 SH2 — no custom windows). Windows: 5y = 2021-05-31 to 2026-05-31; 10y = 2016-05-31 to 2026-05-31, both ending at the funds' published as-at date of 2026-05-31. FX endpoints use BNM 1700-session middle rates on the window boundary dates; 2026-05-31 was a Sunday with no BNM publication, so the window-end rate is the last published trading day 2026-05-29 (3.9680) — a 2-day mismatch versus the fund as-at date, flagged here rather than interpolated. Worked MYR-adjustment arithmetic: MYR-per-USD factor over both windows = end/start = 3.9680/4.1290 = 0.961008 (the ringgit STRENGTHENED ~3.90% against the USD over each window; both windows happen to start at the same published rate of 4.1290). Annualised currency effect = 0.961008^(1/n) - 1: 5y = -0.79%/yr, 10y = -0.40%/yr. MYR-adjusted annualised return = (1 + USD return) x (1 + annualised currency effect) - 1, e.g. QQQ 5y: 1.1792 x 0.992077 - 1 = 16.99%/yr; cross-check in cumulative terms: QQQ 5y cumulative USD = 1.1792^5 - 1 = 128.0% (matches Invesco's own published +127.98% cumulative), in MYR = 2.280 x 0.961008 - 1 = 119.1%, re-annualised = 16.99%/yr. The adjustment direction is window-dependent: over these windows MYR strengthened so the MYR-adjusted return is LOWER than the headline USD return, but a window in which MYR weakens raises it — the point is that the forex variable exists, not that it always cuts one way. KNOWN GAPS (flagged, not estimated): (1) FTSE Russell's freely published KLCI factsheet carries CAPITAL (price) return only — KLCI total return including dividends is not published there, so the KLCI comparator understates what a KLCI-tracking investor earned (the factsheet's point-in-time dividend yield is 3.97% as at 2026-05-29, illustrating the size of the omission; no total-return series was fetched, so none is stated); (2) the factsheet publishes no 10y return at all, so the 10y KLCI row carries null. Both gaps must be restated by any consumer of the KLCI rows.

data.as_at: 2026-05-31 (fund); FX end 2026-05-29 (last BNM publication before window end)
data.source: USD return: Invesco QQQ performance page, Annualized returns as of 05/31/2026, NAV row, 5 year +17.92% (https://www.invesco.com/qqq-etf/en/performance.html, fetched 2026-06-11). FX: BNM 1700-session middle rates 2021-05-31 = 4.1290 and 2026-05-29 = 3.9680 (api.bnm.gov.my). Derived: 1.1792 x (3.9680/4.1290)^(1/5) = 1.1792 x 0.992077 -> 16.99%/yr

USD return: Invesco QQQ performance page, Annualized returns as of 05/31/2026, NAV row, 10 year +21.80% (https://www.invesco.com/qqq-etf/en/performance.html, fetched 2026-06-11). FX: BNM 1700-session middle rates 2016-05-31 = 4.1290 and 2026-05-29 = 3.9680 (api.bnm.gov.my). Derived: 1.2180 x (3.9680/4.1290)^(1/10) = 1.2180 x 0.996031 -> 21.32%/yr

USD return: SSGA SPY fund page, annualised NAV performance as of 2026-05-31, 5 year 14.00% (https://www.ssga.com/us/en/intermediary/etfs/spdr-sp-500-etf-trust-spy, fetched 2026-06-11). FX: BNM 1700-session middle rates 2021-05-31 = 4.1290 and 2026-05-29 = 3.9680 (api.bnm.gov.my). Derived: 1.1400 x 0.992077 -> 13.10%/yr

USD return: SSGA SPY fund page, annualised NAV performance as of 2026-05-31, 10 year 15.49% (https://www.ssga.com/us/en/intermediary/etfs/spdr-sp-500-etf-trust-spy, fetched 2026-06-11). FX: BNM 1700-session middle rates 2016-05-31 = 4.1290 and 2026-05-29 = 3.9680 (api.bnm.gov.my). Derived: 1.1549 x 0.996031 -> 15.03%/yr

data.fund: FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (MYR, capital return — dividends NOT included)
data.source: FTSE Russell FBM KLCI factsheet, data as at 29 May 2026, 'Performance and Volatility - Capital Return' table: Return pa 5YR 1.2% (3YR 6.7%; cumulative 5YR 6.3%, 12M 11.6%) (https://research.ftserussell.com/Analytics/FactSheets/Home/DownloadSingleIssue?issueName=FBMKLCI&IsManual=false, fetched 2026-06-11)
data.gap: Capital (price) return only — the factsheet publishes no total-return table, so this row excludes dividends and understates a KLCI investor's total return; the same factsheet lists the index's dividend yield at 3.97% as at 2026-05-29 (point-in-time characteristic, not a return series). Flag this basis difference wherever this row is shown next to the ETFs' NAV total returns

data.source: FTSE Russell FBM KLCI factsheet, data as at 29 May 2026 — the published performance table stops at 5YR; no 10-year return (capital or total) is published (https://research.ftserussell.com/Analytics/FactSheets/Home/DownloadSingleIssue?issueName=FBMKLCI&IsManual=false, fetched 2026-06-11)
data.gap: No 10y KLCI return is published in the freely available factsheet; left null rather than computed from third-party price archives. Escalated for sourcing (e.g. licensed index data or a Bursa publication) before any 10y KLCI comparison is drawn
